question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection test-on-borrow policy in the pool backing topicRepository.findById() to cause the service to hand out dead connections during a network partition, resulting in query failures that are indistinguishable from legitimate not-found responses?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maximum wait time for connection acquisition in findById() to cause HTTP request handler threads to block indefinitely waiting for a pool connection during a network partition, making the service appear hung rather than degraded?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a network partition detection mechanism in TopicServiceImpl to cause the service to continue accepting new requests during a full DB partition, queuing them all until memory is exhausted, rather than shedding load early?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate the EntityId before passing it to topicRepository.findById() to send crafted IDs that cause the DB to execute expensive index scans, increasing per-query latency and simulating a network partition through query saturation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a connection pool minimum idle setting in the infrastructure backing TopicServiceImpl to cause a cold-pool scenario after a network partition heals, where the first wave of requests all compete to establish new connections simultaneously, re-triggering a partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a DB query statement timeout in findById() to cause long-running SELECT queries on a partitioned DB node to hold locks, blocking write operations and causing a secondary partition in the data ingestion pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl does not implement any load shedding to cause a network partition to be worsened by continued request acceptance, where each new request adds to the backlog of blocked threads and prevents the service from recovering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection pool eviction policy in the infrastructure used by topicRepository.findById() to cause connections that were broken during a network partition to remain in the pool and be handed to new requests, causing a sustained stream of failures after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a request timeout at the HTTP layer above findById() to cause client connections to remain open indefinitely during a network partition, consuming server-side socket descriptors and eventually exhausting the OS file descriptor limit?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not implement any back-pressure mechanism to cause a network partition to propagate upstream to API clients, which then retry aggressively, creating a feedback loop that prevents the partition from healing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a DB connection keepalive setting in the pool used by topicRepository.findById() to cause idle connections to be silently dropped by intermediate network devices during a partial partition, resulting in stale connections being handed to requests that then fail?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a partition-aware error response in findById() to cause automated monitoring systems to misinterpret a network partition as a spike in 404 errors, delaying alerting and allowing the partition to persist beyond the designed recovery window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl uses @RequiredArgsConstructor with a single TopicRepository dependency to cause a network partition in the repository layer to have no fallback path, making the entire service single-point-of-failure dependent on DB connectivity?
